name: manu (first man)
shortDescription: Manu is the progenitor of humanity in Hindu mythology, surviving a great flood and repopulating the earth.
description: In Hindu cosmology, Manu is the archetypal first man and lawgiver. Multiple Manus appear in different cycles (Manvantaras) of time, each presiding over an age and fathering humanity anew after a cataclysmic flood. The most well-known Manu is Vaivasvata Manu, the son of Vivasvan (the Sun God). He is credited with saving humanity from a great deluge by building a boat as instructed by the Matsya avatar of Vishnu. After the flood subsided, Manu offered sacrifices and repopulated the earth with the help of his daughter, Ila. He is also the author of the Manusmriti, a significant legal and social text in Hinduism, which outlines the duties and responsibilities of individuals in various social classes and stages of life. Manu represents the beginning of human civilization and the establishment of dharma (righteous conduct) in each cosmic cycle. He is revered as a wise and virtuous figure who guides humanity towards order and morality, serving as a link between the divine and mortal realms.
